EnergyDesignEENG20006

Solar-Powered Rabeta Boat

All KPIs met in physical prototype — 1800 RPM and 0.2 Nm confirmed by tachometer and Prony brake. Battery-to-shaft efficiency of 74.8% exceeds the gasoline benchmark.

SimulinkMATLABCADMCDABLDC controlBuck converterChain driveLFP sizing

Integrated electro-mechanical system for a solar-powered rabeta boat, designed for Ilha das Onças near Belém, Brazil. Three 390Wp onshore solar panels with MPPT buck converter charging, 48V 9.6kWh LFP battery, 3kW BLDC motor with 2.22:1 chain drive providing 1800 RPM and 16 Nm at the shaft.

Land-based PV installation doubles as community household electricity supply. Total cost £2,657 against £3,000 budget.

Power ElectronicsFEAEENG20005

Ferromagnetic Actuator FEA & Buck Converter

FEA inductance and force curves validated against analytical model across 0.1–5mm airgap. Buck converter output: 11.49V, 208mV ripple.

FEMMMATLABFEAReluctance circuitsBuck converterPCB buildSimulink

Finite-element model of a ferromagnetic actuator across 50 airgap positions. Computed reluctance, inductance, co-energy, and electromagnetic force. FEA nonlinear inductance approximately half the analytical prediction — attributed to B-H saturation at I = 10A.

Synchronous buck converter designed (22V → 12V, 75kHz), inductor wound on ETD29 core, and physically built. Measured output 11.49V with 208mV peak-to-peak ripple.

FEMM model setup
Analytical inductance vs airgap
FEA inductance vs airgap
Design PracticeCADE10001

Line-Following Robot — Engineering Drawing

Produced to engineering-drawing standards — parts list, isometric and sectioned views, and fully dimensioned features. Both the complete robot assembly and the individual part drawings were produced solo.

Technical drawingSectioned viewsDimensioning & tolerancingAssembly
Wheel hub — part drawing

Research technology, media, and telecom companies, assess investment opportunities, and present pitches to the fund's portfolio committee. Building DCF models and sector coverage reports.
